stories.title: Canadian Open Final Round — Suber Chases First PGA Tour Win
stories.angle: Jackson Suber entered the final round at TPC Toronto leading at -13 (one shot over Bud Cauley at -12), seeking his first PGA Tour title. Wyndham Clark charged on Saturday (2 back), along with Tommy Fleetwood and Jesper Svensson. Defending champion Ryan Fox sits at -10. Suber's best previous finish was a T4 at The CJ Cup Byron Nelson three weeks ago. Stakes: first PGA Tour win, full-season exemption, $9.8M purse. Final round underway today.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
FINAL ROUND: RBC Canadian Open 🏌️

Jackson Suber leads at -13 — 1 shot over Bud Cauley. Wyndham Clark, Tommy Fleetwood 2 back. Ryan Fox 3 back.

First PGA Tour win on the line. TPC Toronto, final day. #PGATour #CanadianOpen

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Jackson Suber's best result before today: T4 at The CJ Cup Byron Nelson.

Now he leads the RBC Canadian Open heading into Sunday. One round. First win. Let's go. #CanadianOpen #PGATour

facebook.long_form:
Jackson Suber woke up Sunday morning with one shot between him and his first PGA Tour victory.

The 54-hole leader at the RBC Canadian Open, Suber sits at 13-under par at TPC Toronto at Osprey Valley after shooting rounds of 66-65-66. One stroke separates him from Bud Cauley at 12-under, while Wyndham Clark, Tommy Fleetwood, Brice Garnett, and Jesper Svensson all sit 11-under — two back with 18 holes to play.

Suber's best PGA Tour finish before this week was a tie for fourth at The CJ Cup Byron Nelson just three weeks ago. Now he leads one of the most storied stops on the PGA Tour schedule, a $9.8 million event with defending champion Ryan Fox at 10-under and capable of a charge.

First-time winners on the PGA Tour face a specific kind of pressure on Sunday — the knowledge that years of work can be validated or denied in four hours. Suber has the lead. Can he hold on?

**Who do you think wins the Canadian Open today? Comment below!**
facebook.image_caption: FINAL ROUND: Jackson Suber leads the RBC Canadian Open at -13. One shot. One round. First PGA Tour win on the line. #CanadianOpen #PGATour

article.headline: Canadian Open Final Round: Suber Leads at -13 With First PGA Tour Win in Sight

stories.title: US Open Preview — Scheffler's Grand Slam Bid at Shinnecock, Four Days Away
stories.angle: The 2026 US Open at Shinnecock Hills Golf Club in Southampton, New York starts Thursday June 18 — four days away. Scottie Scheffler (+550) is the favorite in his quest to complete the career Grand Slam. He turns 30 on June 21, which is also the final round — Father's Day at Shinnecock. McIlroy (+900) hunting a seventh major. Par 70, 7,400 yards, brutal greens and 5-inch rough. Jon Rahm (+1400), Xander Schauffele (+1600) among contenders.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
4 days until the 2026 US Open at Shinnecock Hills.

Scottie Scheffler +550 — hunting the career Grand Slam.

He turns 30 on the final round. Father's Day. At Shinnecock.

The story writes itself. #USOpen #Golf

facebook.long_form:
Four days from now, the 2026 US Open begins at Shinnecock Hills Golf Club in Southampton, New York. And it is framed around one of the most compelling individual stories in professional golf.

Scottie Scheffler — the world's No. 1 player, the favorite at +550 — arrives at Shinnecock needing only one thing to complete the career Grand Slam: a US Open title. He already holds The Masters, The Open Championship, and the PGA Championship. One trophy, one major, separates him from a company of legends.

The course will not make it easy. Shinnecock Hills last hosted the US Open in 2018, when Brooks Koepka defeated Tommy Fleetwood by one stroke over a Par 70 layout measuring around 7,400 yards with rough that reaches five inches. The USGA specializes in setups that expose the slightest weakness — and at Shinnecock, there are no hiding places.

Rory McIlroy (+900) arrives hunting a seventh major title. Jon Rahm (+1400), Xander Schauffele (+1600), Tommy Fleetwood, Cameron Young, Bryson DeChambeau, and Ludvig Aberg all enter as contenders.

But the story is Scheffler. It always will be this week.

**Who is your pick for the 2026 US Open? Drop your prediction below!**
facebook.image_caption: US Open starts Thursday at Shinnecock Hills 🏌️ Scheffler +550. Grand Slam bid. Can he do it? #USOpen #Golf

article.headline: US Open 2026 Preview: Scheffler's Grand Slam Bid, Shinnecock Hills, and Four Days of Anticipation

stories.title: LIV Golf Crisis — Saudi Arabia Ending Funding, Events Cancelled
stories.angle: PIF (Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund) has funded only $196M of the $600M LIV Golf needs for the 2026 season ($66M in May + $130M in June). CEO Greg O'Neil cannot guarantee the final events. LIV Indianapolis and Team Championship Michigan confirmed at risk. Louisiana already postponed. LIV UK and LIV NY expected to proceed. Saudi Arabia announced it will end LIV funding after 2026. LIV seeking $250-350M from new investors for "LIV 2.0" plan.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
LIV Golf has received $196M of the $600M it needs to finish the 2026 season.

CEO can't guarantee final events. Louisiana postponed. Saudi Arabia ending funding.

This isn't a bad week for LIV. This is an existential moment. #LIVGolf #Golf

facebook.long_form:
LIV Golf is facing its most serious existential moment since the league launched.

Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund (PIF) — the financial engine behind LIV Golf since its creation — has provided $196 million of the roughly $600 million the league requires to complete the 2026 season. That leaves a shortfall of more than $400 million, and CEO Greg O'Neil has publicly acknowledged that he cannot guarantee the final events on the schedule.

Louisiana has already been postponed. LIV Indianapolis and the LIV Team Championship in Michigan are at risk of cancellation. LIV UK and LIV New York are currently expected to proceed, but the broader instability is undeniable.

The deeper news is even more significant: PIF governor Yasir Al-Rumayyan has stepped down as LIV Golf's chairman, and Saudi Arabia has announced it will end funding for LIV Golf after the 2026 season concludes. The league is now actively seeking $250 to $350 million from new private investors to fund a revamped "LIV 2.0" plan.

The circuit that disrupted professional golf and generated billions in controversy may not outlast this season in its current form.

**What do you think the future of LIV Golf looks like?**
facebook.image_caption: LIV Golf funding crisis: $196M received of $600M needed. Saudi Arabia ending support. Events at risk. What's next? #LIVGolf #Golf

article.headline: LIV Golf Crisis: Saudi Arabia Ends Funding After 2026, League Races to Find New Investors

stories.title: Dow LPGA Championship — Somi Lee and Jin Hee Im Win at 20-Under
stories.angle: The Dow LPGA Championship (best-ball format, 72 teams, $3.3M) at Midland Country Club concluded with Somi Lee and Jin Hee Im winning at 20-under (260). They shot a bogey-free final round of 62, with eight birdies, to claim the title. The event ran June 8-14.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Somi Lee and Jin Hee Im win the 2026 Dow LPGA Championship at 20-under! 🏆

Final round: 62. Bogey-free. 8 birdies.

Dominant performance to claim the title at Midland Country Club. #LPGATour #DowChampionship

facebook.long_form:
The 2026 Dow LPGA Championship had a dominant finish.

Somi Lee and Jin Hee Im captured the title at Midland Country Club in Michigan, posting a 20-under total of 260 to claim the $3.3 million event. Their final round was a 62 — completely bogey-free, with eight birdies — a clinic in consistent excellence over 18 holes.

The Dow Championship uses a unique best-ball format, pairing 72 two-person teams in a competition that rewards consistent ball-striking and smart partner management. Lee and Im made it look straightforward on Sunday, closing out from the front with a round that gave their competitors no chance to catch them.

The tournament ran June 8-14 at Midland Country Club, the same Michigan venue that has hosted the event in recent years.

**Congratulations to Somi Lee and Jin Hee Im on a fantastic week!**
facebook.image_caption: Somi Lee and Jin Hee Im WIN the 2026 Dow LPGA Championship at 20-under! Final round: 62, bogey-free. 🏆 #LPGATour #DowChampionship

article.headline: Dow LPGA Championship: Somi Lee and Jin Hee Im Win at 20-Under With Dominant Final Round

stories.title: Scheffler's Birthday Major — Turning 30 on Father's Day at Shinnecock
stories.angle: Scottie Scheffler has the opportunity to complete the career Grand Slam at Shinnecock Hills — and if he wins the US Open on Sunday June 21, it will be his 30th birthday and Father's Day simultaneously. The story writes itself: the world's No. 1 player, turning 30, playing his best golf, on Father's Day, at one of America's most challenging courses. A win would place him among the all-time greats. He is the +550 favorite to make it happen.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Scottie Scheffler's US Open final round (June 21):

His 30th birthday. Father's Day. Career Grand Slam on the line.

+550 to win at Shinnecock.

There is no better sports story in golf right now. #USOpen #Scheffler

facebook.long_form:
The calendar has lined up in a way that seems almost impossible to believe.

Scottie Scheffler — the world's best golfer, the current favorite for the 2026 US Open at +550 — will play the final round of the US Open at Shinnecock Hills on Sunday, June 21. That is his 30th birthday. It is also Father's Day.

If Scheffler wins the US Open, he completes the career Grand Slam — joining Tiger Woods, Jack Nicklaus, Ben Hogan, Gary Player, Gene Sarazen, and a small handful of others who have won all four professional majors. He already has The Masters, The Open Championship, and the PGA Championship. One win stands between him and golf immortality.

And the universe has arranged it so that the win — if it comes — arrives on the day he turns 30 and celebrates his first Father's Day.

Golf has produced some extraordinary storylines. This is one of the great ones.

**Do you think Scheffler can do it at Shinnecock? Drop your prediction below.**
facebook.image_caption: Scheffler's US Open final round = his 30th birthday + Father's Day. Grand Slam on the line. +550. This is the story of the week. #Scheffler #USOpen

article.headline: Scheffler's Birthday Major: Turning 30 on Father's Day While Hunting the Grand Slam at Shinnecock
